title: UNICEF: 6.5 Million Somalis Face Acute Hunger, Iran-War Supply Chains Add New Risk
detail: UNICEF Executive Director Catherine Russell visited Dollow in the Gedo region and issued an emergency warning that Somalia's humanitarian crisis is dramatically worsening: 6.5 million people — one-third of Somalia's population — face IPC Phase 3–4 crisis-level hunger as of March 2026, up 1.7 million from January. Some 1.84 million children under five are expected to suffer acute malnutrition in 2026, with roughly 500,000 at severe risk. Russell warned that the ongoing Iran-Israel war has disrupted global humanitarian supply chains, potentially raising transit costs for lifesaving supplies by 30–60% and placing $15.7 million in therapeutic food, vaccines, and mosquito nets in transit uncertainty. The 2025 Deyr cereal harvest was 83% below the 30-year average — the worst in three decades. WFP can currently reach only 1 in 10 people in need and requires $95 million urgently to continue through August 2026. Somalia's total 2026 humanitarian funding requirement stands at $852 million; the EU has allocated €63 million.

title: AFRICOM Airstrike Targets Al-Shabaab ~132 km Northwest of Kismayo
detail: US Africa Command (AFRICOM) conducted a confirmed airstrike targeting Al-Shabaab approximately 132 km northwest of Kismayo, in the Jubbaland region of southern Somalia. AFRICOM Press Release #36323 confirmed the strike and stated it was conducted in coordination with the Federal Government of Somalia. No casualty assessment was released per standard operational security policy. The strike is part of a dramatically intensified 2026 air campaign: by early March 2026, the US had already conducted at least 43 airstrikes in Somalia — on pace to exceed the 2025 annual record of 124 total strikes. The Trump administration has dramatically expanded the Somalia air campaign relative to the 11 strikes recorded in all of 2024 under Biden, acting under an expanded counter-terrorism mandate authorizing direct strikes against both Al-Shabaab and ISIS-Somalia.

title: EU NAVFOR Atalanta Locates Hijacked Dhow 480 Nautical Miles Southeast of Mogadishu
detail: An EU NAVFOR Operation Atalanta vessel located a hijacked dhow approximately 480 nautical miles southeast of Mogadishu and confirmed its identity. The response followed a piracy event reported on March 25. No further details on crew status or vessel resolution were immediately available. The incident is part of a significant resurgence of Somali piracy: Pirate Action Groups (PAGs) are operating 300–800 nautical miles offshore using hijacked dhows as motherships. Piracy incidents increased approximately 110% in 2025 per the MICA annual report. All vessels within 700 nautical miles of the Somali coast are advised to follow BMP5 protocols and register with MSCHOA.
